def: v.t. To tune the voice; to run over the notos provious to singing; to catch the proper key; to pitch or raise the voice; — to cry (with pain), moan, groan (syn. allānā).
v.t. To tune the voice; to run over the notos provious to singing; to catch the proper key; to pitch or raise the voice; — to cry (with pain), moan, groan (syn. allānā).
adj. & s.f. = آلاريسی ālāresī, q.v. adj. Slack, careless, slovenly, remiss, languid, heedless, indifferent; — s.f. Carelessness, heedlessness, indifference, &c.
adj. & s.f. = آلاريسی ālāresī, q.v. adj. Slack, careless, slovenly, remiss, languid, heedless, indifferent; — s.f. Carelessness, heedlessness, indifference, &c.

ety: said to be fr. A. allāh+S. r̤ishī, — devotees being regarded as lax and neglectful in respect of earthly matters
def: adj. Slack, careless, slovenly, remiss, languid, heedless, indifferent; — s.f. Carelessness, heedlessness, indifference, &c.

ety: S. आलस्य and आलस, from अ not+लस् 'to labour'
def: s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.

ety: S. आलस्य and आलस, from अ not+लस् 'to labour'
def: s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀆𑀮𑀲𑁆𑀲 (ālassa), from Sanskrit आलस्य (ā́lasya). Cognate with Assamese এলাহ (elah) and Marathi आळस (āḷas).

def: adj. Inactive, lazy, indolent, slothful; drowsy; weak; — s.m. A lazy person, sluggard, &c.
(for a-lastu), Am I not? — The phrase is borrowed from Sūra vii. v. 168 of the Qorʼān: ʻa-lastu bi-rabbi-kum?'
s.m. = ālas, q.v. s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.
s.m. A firobrand. (cf. alāʼo.)
s.f. Tool, instrument, implement, apparatus; tackle, rigging (of a ship); utensil; — membrum virile, penis.
s.f. pl. (of آلت alat), Instruments, tools, implements, apparatus, tackling; utensils.
adj. Not of this world, supernatural, super-human, transcendental; unknown, uncommon, strange, rare, extraordinary, not current; unpopular.
s.f. Eaves of a house ( = oltī, orī. the com. form, q.q.v.). s.f. Eaves of a thatched house ( = orī, q.v.)
subs: To be utterly stupefied or intoxicated with bhang.

def: s.m. A bonfire; a fire kindled in a hole in the ground, round which the villagers sit and warm themselves, and, in parts of India, around which Moḥammadans dance in the festival of Moḥarram.
s.m. A descendant of ʻAlī (Moḥammad's son-in-law).
s.m. House, habitation, abode, place.
s.m. A basin for water round the root of a tree.
adj. Strong enough, having sufficient power; omnipotent; — s.m. Greatest strength or power, omnipotence.
adv. v.s.v. al, 'the.'
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَلْبَتَّه (albatta), from Arabic الْبَتَّةَ (al-battata).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Playful, sportive, wanton; capricious, fanciful, wayward; volatile, sprightly, lively, gay; light, frivolous, senseless, childish, silly; simple, naïve, artless; — free, independent; careless, indifferent; — strange, uncommon, extraordinary, singular, peculiar; — smart, showy, spruce, foppish; engaging, charming, handsome, beautiful
subs: (f. -ī), A wanton, or capricious, &c. man (or woman); a beau, fop; voluptuary, rake, roué; simpleton, fool, noodle
s.f. A fast woman; a belle; a silly woman, &c.

def: s.m. Playfulness, sportiveness, wantonness, sprightnness, gayety, frolic; capriciousness, waywardness; giddiness, frivolity, levity, silliness, childishness; simplicity, artlessness, innocence; — carelessness, indifference; strangeness, oddness, singularity, peculiarity; — smartness, spruceness, foppishness; airs, blandishments, coquettishness; charm, fascination; beauty, prettiness, elegance.

s.m. Playfulness, sportiveness, wantonness, sprightnness, gayety, frolic; capriciousness, waywardness; giddiness, frivolity, levity, silliness, childishness; simplicity, artlessness, innocence; — carelessness, indifference; strangeness, oddness, singularity, peculiarity; — smartness, spruceness, foppishness; airs, blandishments, coquettishness; charm, fascination; beauty, prettiness, elegance.

ety: S. अलम्+वृत्; but cf. albelā, to which albeṭ may be related
def: s.f. Turn, twist, bend, winding; (cf. lapeṭ)

adj. & s.m. = albelā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Playful, sportive, wanton; capricious, fanciful, wayward; volatile, sprightly, lively, gay; light, frivolous, senseless, childish, silly; simple, naïve, artless; — free, independent; careless, indifferent; — strange, uncommon, extraordinary, singular, peculiar; — smart, showy, spruce, foppish; engaging, charming, handsome, beautiful
subs: (f. -ī), A wanton, or capricious, &c. man (or woman); a beau, fop; voluptuary, rake, roué; simpleton, fool, noodle
s.f. A fast woman; a belle; a silly woman, &c.

def: adj. & adv. Foolish enough, very foolish or ridiculous, senseless, absurd; useless, fruitless, vain.

ety: burz = Zend barez; from barez = S. barh, cf. al-wand
def: s.m. The highest mountain in the Caucasus; also other high mountains in Hamadān, &c.
v.n. To cry out, scream, bawl, &c.
s.m. Humming and hawing; putting off, shuffling, evasion, subterfuge, deception, fraud, deceit; — procrastination; inactivity, idleness, sloth
subs: v.t. To put off, shuffle, evade; to deceive, delude, trick.

def: adj. That cannot be written; that cannot be taken account of.
adj. Careless, indifferent, heedless, thoughtless, inconsiderate, imprudent, improvident.
s.m. Liniment; plaster.
adj. Thousand. (Syn. hazār.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الف (alif), from Arabic أَلِف (ʔalif).

def: s.m. A kind of shirt without sleeves and open at the sides, worn by faqīrs. (It is so called because of the alifs marked on it.)
s.m. pl. (of لفظ lafẕ), Words, articulate sounds, terms; technical terms.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَلْفَاظ (alfāz), from Arabic أَلْفَاظ (ʔalfāẓ).

def: adj. Covered with marks like the letter alif (cloth, &c.); — s.f. dim. of alfā, q.v.
adj. Separate, apart, distinct; free, loose, unconfined, &c. (see alag): — s.m. A sandal.
s.f. Separation; holding or keeping aloof, non-interference; being silent.
v.t. = alag karnā, q.v.s.v. alag; to put on one side; to put up, raise.
s.m. Corr. of alg̠oza, q.v.
s.m. Pipe, flute; whistle; a small flageolet.
s.m. The name of a certain Hindū hero, &c. = ālā, q.v.
s.f. The name of a rāginī or musical mode.
s.f. pl. (of لحن laḥn), Notes, tunes, melodies, &c. (see next).
v.n. To rejoice; to be cheerful.
s.m. A large black bee, the bumble bee ( = alī); a crow; the Indian cuckoo, Cuculus indicus; a scorpion; spirituous liquor.
s.m. The bumble bee, &c. (see ali); — s.f. A female friend, &c. (see ālī).

ety: S. अवर्ति, ava+r̤iti, rt. r̤i; cf. the H. form आवली
def: s.f. Misfortune; great danger; risk of life. (cf. alīk and alīh,' 'an unpleasant thing.')
s.m. A large black bee, the bumble-bee; spirituous liquor; — s.f. A woman's female friend ( = ālī).
adj. Of or belonging to the āl tree (see āl)
subs: s.m. A colour extracted from the al tree, āl-colour.

def: adj. High (in rank, &c.), eminent, noble; — s.m. The name of Moḥammad's son-in-law (he was, according to the Sunnī sect, the fourth Khalīfa or successor of Moḥammad; but the Shīʻas make him the direct successor, not acknowledging the three other Caliphs); a proper name
subs: s.m. ʻAlī's tie'; an amulet or charm (against sorceries or enchantments); an ornament, a jewel
s.f. 'Alī's help'; a return salutation; — name of a trick in wrestling.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَلِی (alī), from Arabic عَلِيّ (ʕaliyy).

def: s.m. The bumble-bee; a scorpion ( = ali); — s.f. A woman's female friend; — row, range, continuous line; bed (of flowers or vegetables); ridge, mound of earth crossing ditches or dividing fields ( = meṅṛ), dike, embankment; — a land measure, equivalent to four bīsīs.

ety: for A. عالٍ ʻāliṉ, act. part. of علو 'to be high,' &c.
def: adj. High, elevated, exalted, eminent, noble, grand, sublime
subs: adj. Of high descent, of noble lineage
or ʻālī-janāb, adj. Of high rank, of exalted dignity; — your Highness, your Excellency
s.f. Highness of rank or dignity
adj. Of a high family, of a noble stock
adj. Of high rank or dignity; magnificent, splendid; stately, grand, majestic
or ʻālī-martaba, or ʻālī-maqām, or ʻālī-miqdār, or ʻālī-manqabat, or ālī-makān, adj. Of high rank or dignity
or ʻālī-himmat, adj. High-minded, magnanimous; of an aspiring nature, ambitious
or ʻālī-himmatī, s.f. High-mindedness, magnanimity; ambition.
wety: From Arabic عَالٍ (ʕālin).

def: s.m. The first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets
subs: 'The alif of (the word) allāh'; adj. Alone, friendless, having no friend save God; destitute, poor
The mark of the letter alif made by faqīrs on the forehead (indicative of their separation from worldly concerns, &c.)
s.f. The a, b, c, the alphabet
v.n. To become erect; to rear (as a horse).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الف (alif), from Arabic أَلِف (ʔalif).

def: s.m. = alīk and alī, q.q.v. adj. False, untrue; unpleasant, displeasing; — s.m. Falsehood, untruth; anything unpleasant or displeasing.
adj. False, untrue; unpleasant, displeasing; — s.m. Falsehood, untruth; anything unpleasant or displeasing.
adj. Weak; sick, indisposed, out of sorts.
adj. Painful, torturing, excruciating.
adj. & s.m. Knowing, learned, intelligent, wise, sage; — a learned man, a doctor (of law, or religion, &c.), one versed in science and literature
subs: or ʻālim-ě-g̠aib, adj. & s.m. Prescient, knowing the past and the future, or the visible and the invisible; omniscient; — the One who knows the visible and the invisible, the Omniscient.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَالَم (ālam), borrowed from Arabic عَالَم (ʕālam), borrowed from Aramaic עָלְמָא or Classical Syriac ܥܳܠܡܳܐ.

def: adj. Learned, wise ( = ʻālim); omniscient, an epithet applied to God.

ety: ʻālim+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Learned, wise, sage; — wisely, like a sage.
s.m. The bumble bee = alī, q.v. s.m. A large black bee, the bumble bee ( = alī); a crow; the Indian cuckoo, Cuculus indicus; a scorpion; spirituous liquor.
adj. Not melted or dissolved; not effaced, not wiped out or away; not embraced; not attached to, unconnected.
s.m. Embracing, clasping in the arms; embrace
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit आलिङ्गन (āliṅgana). Compare Gujarati આલિંગન (āliṅgan), Marathi आलिंगन (āliṅgan), Kannada ಆಲಿಂಗನ (āliṅgana), Telugu ఆలింగనము (āliṅganamu), Malayalam ആലിംഗനം (āliṅganaṁ), Maithili आलिङ्गन (āliṅgan) / 𑒂𑒪𑒱𑒓𑓂𑒑𑒢 (āliṅgana), Bengali আলিঙ্গন (aliṅgon), Assamese আলিঙ্গন (aliṅgon), Sinhalese ආලිංගනය (āliṁganaya).

def: adj. Embracing; — s.m. One who embraces.
v.t. To come and surround; to fall suddenly upon.
s.m. Name of a tribe of banjāras.
(for A. عالية, fem. of ʻālī), adj. f. = ʻālī, q.v. s.m. A large black bee, the bumble bee ( = alī); a crow; the Indian cuckoo, Cuculus indicus; a scorpion; spirituous liquor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَالِیَه (āliya), from Arabic عَالِيَة (ʕāliya).

def: s.m. Bad or terrifying dream, nightmare.
s.m. A disputatious or quarrelsome person, a wrangler.
s.m. Forms of address; the address (of a letter).
adj. Speaking barbarously; stammering, stuttering (cf. luknat).
s.f. Inactivity, sluggishness, sloth, &c. = ālas, q.v. (cf. askat). s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.
v.n. = alsānā, q.v. v.n. To be slack, slow, sluggish, lazy; to doze, be drowsy or sleepy; to rest; to be fatigued, wearied, done up.
s.f. Inactivity, sluggishness, sloth, &c. = ālas, q.v. (cf. askat). s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.
s.f. = ālkas, q.v.
subs: &c. = ālsī and askatī, q.q.v. s.f. Inactivity, sluggishness, sloth, &c. = ālas, q.v. (cf. askat). s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.

def: s.m. Cutting off, severing, sundering; adj. Put aside, dismissed; rejected; inadmissible
subs: v.t. To cut off, sever, sunder, break off; to cut short, bring to an end; to put away, dismiss, get rid of; to adjust, settle, balance, wind up (an account, &c.).

def: s.f. A coat with sleeves, a surtout (like the qabā, but having buttons, instead of strings, at the neck, breast, and navel. — Some, however, affirm that the garment is worn under the qabā.

ety: i.e. al ilāh, 'the God,' by way of eminence
def: s.m. God, the Supreme Being; — intj. O God! Have mercy!
subs: God is great (an introductory ejaculation used in prayer, and also when slaughtering an animal; and an exclamation of surprise, wonder, resignation, &c.: it is called the takbīr)
intj. (expressive of surprise, approbation, &c.), Good God! good gracious! wonderful! extraordinary! excellent! thank God!
To call on God; to say (one's) prayers, count (one's) beads
intj. God grant it be so! So be it! Heaven protect him; — adv. With prayer and fasting, &c.; with great care, anxiously; — allāh āmīn-kā laṛkā, A child granted after many prayers
(for allāh-wālī), intj. God preserve you! God speed! Adieu!
To propitiate God and the saints (by offerings, &c.); to make a religious vow
The name of God; — nothing whatever, not a rap
To take the name of God, to have the fear of God (in one, — said to one who tells a lie, e.g. allāh-kā nām lo!)
lit. 'The light of God'; a gift from God (as a beard, &c); a very beautiful person; (ironically) a hideously ugly person; a very wicked person
An innocent, a demented person, lunatic; simpleton, fool
The soul; life; the animal kingdom; — a poor fellow, poor creature
allāh-log, s.m. God's people, holy men, good men; mendicants; careless or thoughtless persons; — simple-minded people, guileless people
s.f. lit. 'One stricken by God'; A God-forsaken wife; a gadding wife; an unchaste woman, a harlot
s.f. An artless, simple-minded person; a simpleton; — a snail (Helix)
intj. O God! God alone is stable and sure! Good God! (an ejaculation expressing surprise, &c. = allāh re); — a salutation among faqīrs.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَللّٰه (allāh), from Arabic اللّٰه (allāh). First attested as Old Hindi अलह (alaha).

ety: intens. n. fr. علم; see ʻilm
def: adj. Very knowing, very scientific, very learned; all-knowing, omniscient; — s.m. The Omniscient
subs: adj. & s.m. Knowing all mysteries, acquainted with things invisible, an epithet of the Deity.

ety: for A. علامة, fr. ʻallām, q.v.
def: adj. Very knowing, most learned ( = ʻallām); — s.m. & f. A very learned man or woman; — a very knowing or artful woman
subs: (also ʻallāmatuʼd-dahr), or ʻallāma-e-aṣr (also ʻallāmatuʼl-ʻaṣr), s.m. The most learned of the age.

def: s.m. Nonsense, rubbish, trash; toying, frivolity; pretext, idle excuse; misappropriation, embezzlement; — adv. Nonsensically, without consideration, without discrimination, at random
subs: v.n. To talk nonsense, to prate absurdly, or at random
v.t. To misappropriate embezzle; to hocus-pocus
v.t. To eat all kinds of trash.

ety: A. الل alal, rt. الّ alla; cf. H. alalānā
def: v.n. To cry out (with pain), to groan, wail; to bawl, scream, shriek.

ety: S. अलम्+लल; — cf. the form allaṛh
def: adj. Boyish, childish; young; raw, inexperienced, inexpert; thoughtless; untaught, ignorant; untrained, undisciplined; — s.m. Youth, child, a mere boy (in contempt); young (of an animal), colt, &c.
subs: s.f. Childish talk, silly prattle; childishness, folly; trifling employment; frivolity.

def: adj. = allaṛ, q.v. adj. Boyish, childish; young; raw, inexperienced, inexpert; thoughtless; untaught, ignorant; untrained, undisciplined; — s.m. Youth, child, a mere boy (in contempt); young (of an animal), colt, &c.
subs: s.f. Childish talk, silly prattle; childishness, folly; trifling employment; frivolity.

def: adj. & s.m. = allaṛ or allaṛh, q.v. adj. Boyish, childish; young; raw, inexperienced, inexpert; thoughtless; untaught, ignorant; untrained, undisciplined; — s.m. Youth, child, a mere boy (in contempt); young (of an animal), colt, &c.
subs: s.f. Childish talk, silly prattle; childishness, folly; trifling employment; frivolity.

def: s.f. Chest of drawers; book-case, press, wardrobe, cabinet.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese almario or armário, from Latin armarium (“closet, chest”), from arma (“weapons, tools”) + -ārium (“denoting a place where things are kept”).

def: s.m. Diamond; adamant
subs: s.m. Diamond-cutter; a diamond used in cutting other gems or glass; diamond-dust; — adj. Cut into angles or facets (as glass, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الماس (almās).

def: adj. Cut into facets (like a diamond) of, or resembling, diamond
subs: s.m. Diamond colour; a bright, clear, white colour.

def: adj. Intoxicated, drunk; — lustful, lascivious, wanton; — infuriated; mad, insane.
s.m. A portion of unripe corn.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀆𑀮𑀼𑀕 (āluga), from Sanskrit आलुक (āluka, “elephant foot yam”). The word began to be used in the sense "potato" after the importation of potatoes to South Asia from the Americas by Europeans, displacing cultivation of the similar elephant foot yam.

def: s.m. Absence of, or freedom from, cupidity or covetousness; contentment.
s.m. Sight, seeing, looking; appearance; — light; flattery, panegyric.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: s.m. Disappearance; invisibility.
s.m. Looking, seeing; sight.
s.m. Gambol (especially of a horse), caper, frisking; frolic, playfulness; wantonness, dalliance, toying
subs: To gambol, caper, frisk, skip, frolic

def: adj. Not shaking, steady, firm.
adj. = alaukik, q.v. adj. Not of this world, supernatural, super-human, transcendental; unknown, uncommon, strange, rare, extraordinary, not current; unpopular.
adj. Without salt; insufficiently salted; fresh; tasteless, insipid.
part. adj. Disappeared; concealed, hidden; retired; out of sight; invisible, unseen, imperceptible, undiscernible; run out; defaced, destroyed
subs: s.m. A collyrium, which, applied to the eyes of any one, renders him or her invisible
s.f. The mysteries of nature; the invisible power in nature
v.n. To be concealed; to become invisible; to disappear, vanish, retire, get out of sight; to cease to be, become extinct.

def: Not concealed or hidden; visible, apparent.
v.t. To conceal, hide; — v.n. To lie hid, be concealed.
v.t. To stir, mix; to churn ( = bilonā, q.v.). v.t. To stir, shake, agitate, to churn.
s.m. A screen ( = oṭ, q.v.). s.f. Anything to be gained, advantage, gain, profit; overplus, surplus, saving; abatement, economy; recovery (from sickness), convalescence
subs: Profit or benefit to accrue, to be gained

def: adj. Small, minute, trifling; little; few; short
subs: (alpa+āhāra), adj. Taking little food, moderate in eating, abstemious, abstinent; — s.m. Moderation in eating, abstinence; — a mode rate eater
(alpa+āyus), adj. Short-lived, of few years, of little age, of short duration, young; — s.m.f. A young person; an ephemeral creature
s.m. (in Gram.) An unaspirated consonant (as k, g)
adj. Of little consequence or weight, insignificant
adj. Of little weight or measure; of little authority; resting on little evidence
(alpajna), adj. Knowing little, ignorant; — s.m. (f. -ā), A person of defective, meagre, or limited knowledge
adj. Of little strength, weak ( = alp-bal)
adj. Short-winded; — s.m. A short-winded person
s.m. A little, a little merely; a short time, a few moments
adj. Of little price, of small value
alpa-yaś, adj. Of little note, not known to fame, obscure; — s.m. Limited notoriety or fame.

def: s.f. = S الپتو अल्पत्व alpatwa, s.m. Smallness, insignificance, inferiority, paucity.
s.m. = S الپتا अल्पता alpatā, s.f. Smallness, insignificance, inferiority, paucity.
s.m. A pin.
adj. Inactive; apathetic; drowsy; used up.
v.n. To be slack, slow, sluggish, lazy; to doze, be drowsy or sleepy; to rest; to be fatigued, wearied, done up.
Idleness, &c. = ālas, q.v. (The forms would appear to be peculiar to Southern and Western India.)
Idleness, &c. = ālas, q.v. (The forms would appear to be peculiar to Southern and Western India.)
Idleness, &c. = ālas, q.v. (The forms would appear to be peculiar to Southern and Western India.)

ety: prob. by Met. for laseṭ, S. लस+इत
def: s.f. Astuteness, sharpness, knavery, roguery, trickery, humbug, gammon, duplicity, deception, fraud; illicit connection, liaison; deficiency (in an account).

adj. & s.m. Astute, artful, crafty; deceitful, fraudulent, backward in paying debts; — lazy, indolent, slothful (these meanings point to alsaṭ as the source); — An artful, crafty fellow, &c.; a lazy rascal.
s.f. Linseed, Linum usitatissimum ( = tīsī)
s.f. pl. (of لسان lisān), Tongues, languages.
s.m. Slothfulness, &c. = ālas, q.v. s.m. Inactivity, lack of energy, indolence, laziness, idleness, sloth; drowsiness; — adj. Lazy, slothful, indolent; drowsy.
s.m. Lac-dye; Cotton strongly impregnated with lac-dye, ready to be used for dyeing (used by Hindū women for staining their feet red).
s.m. Lac-dye; Cotton strongly impregnated with lac-dye, ready to be used for dyeing (used by Hindū women for staining their feet red).
s.m. pl. (of لطف lut̤f,), Kindnesses, favours.
s.m. The royal seal; a grant of land under the royal seal in perpetuity descending to posterity; royal insignia, diploma; a medal; — a tax levied on travellers.
s.m. The royal seal; a grant of land under the royal seal in perpetuity descending to posterity; royal insignia, diploma; a medal; — a tax levied on travellers.

ety: S. अरति? 'moving quickly,' rt. ऋ+nī from न
def: s.f. The rope round the neck of an elephant in which the driver puts his feet, as in stirrups.
s.m. Plum, prune
subs: s.m. A peach, lit. 'the rough plum.'
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀆𑀮𑀼𑀕 (āluga), from Sanskrit आलुक (āluka, “elephant foot yam”). The word began to be used in the sense "potato" after the importation of potatoes to South Asia from the Americas by Europeans, displacing cultivation of the similar elephant foot yam.

def: s.m. An esculent root, Arum campanulatum; potato, Solanum tuberosum
subs: s.m. The potato-plant; the tapioca shrub.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀆𑀮𑀼𑀕 (āluga), from Sanskrit आलुक (āluka, “elephant foot yam”). The word began to be used in the sense "potato" after the importation of potatoes to South Asia from the Americas by Europeans, displacing cultivation of the similar elephant foot yam.

def: s.m. A kind of small plum, Prunus ovalifolia.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آلُوچَه (ālūča).

def: part. = ālūda, q.v. (The form ālūd occurs, generally, in comp.) part. adj. Defiled, polluted, sullied, soiled, stained, spoiled; smeared, immersed, covered; loaded (with), overwhelmed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آلود (ālūd).

ety: perf. part. of ālūdan; ā+rt. Z. rā = S. रा and ला
def: part. adj. Defiled, polluted, sullied, soiled, stained, spoiled; smeared, immersed, covered; loaded (with), overwhelmed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آلوده (ālūda), past participle of آلودن (ālūdan, “to pollute”).

def: s.f. Contamination, defilement, pollutedness; pollution, stain; foulness, impurity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آلُودَگِی (ālūdagī). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi آلودگی (ālvdgy /⁠ālūdagī⁠/).

def: part. adj. Astonished, demented, mad (with love, &c.), crazy ( = āshufta); — s.m. Reprobate, dissolute fellow; worthless companion. (Syn. rind.)
s.m. A wild plum; a sloe.
s.m. pl. (of لوح lauḥ), Tablets, tables; planks.
s.m. Basin for water round the foot of a tree (v. ālbāl).
adj. Of diverse kinds; of various colours
subs: s m. Blessings of various kinds; various kinds of good things; different sorts of dishes.

ety: Pehl. arvand; Zend aurvañt, fr. ar; S. arvant
def: s.m. A mountain in the province of Hamadān, in Persia.
adj. & adv. Soft, gentle, tender; light; — softly, &c.; with a light hand, lightly
s.m. Mango, the fruit of the Mangifera indica; also the tree

am-ras, s.m. Inspissated juice of the mango.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀁𑀩 (aṃba), from Sanskrit आम्र (āmrá).

def: adj. Raw, uncooked, undressed; unripe; undigested; — s.m. Condition of being raw, &c.; provisions, victuals (undressed); sickness, disease, (especially) disease in the bowels, the mucous matter voided in dysentery ( = āṅw, the common form)
subs: (S. āma+anna), s.m. Undressed rice, raw grain; uncooked provisions
(S. āma+vāta), s.m. Constipation or torpor of the bowels with flatulence and intumescence; — a windy tumour, a swelling (without pain)
s.m. Pain arising from indigestion; the colic.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀁𑀩 (aṃba), from Sanskrit आम्र (āmrá).

def: v.n. To be exhausted, worn out, spent, used up.
adj. = اعمي aʻmā, q.v.
adj. Blind; ignorant; — s.m. A desert.
adj. Raw, &c. = ām, q.v. s.m. Mango, the fruit of the Mangifera indica; also the tree
subs: s.f. A tope or grove of mango trees; — income or revenue obtained from mango groves and gar dens
am-ras, s.m. Inspissated juice of the mango.

def: s.m. pl. (of měʻā), Intestines, guts.

ety: v.n. of āmadan; ā+Pehl. gmatan, rt. Zend gam = S. गम्
def: s.m. Coming, approach, arrival; access; coming in, income; receipts; — adj. Coming naturally, natural, unartificial, not studied (opp. to āward); plain, simple
subs: s.f. The announcement of an arrival
āmad o raft, s.f. Coming and going; ingress and egress, communication, intercourse; passage, way, thoroughfare
s.m. One who has a large income, a rich man; a merchant who purchases imported goods wholesale.

def: part. (of āmadan, 'to come'). Come, arrived.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آمده (āmada).

ety: perf. part. of āmādan; ā+rt. Zend mā = S. मा
def: part. adj. Prepared, ready, alert; disposed (to)
subs: v.t. To get ready, to prepare; to rouse, stir up, incite, excite; to offer to do; to abet
v.n. To be prepared (for), get ready; to be on the alert; to be disposed (to).

def: s.f. The state of being prepared, readiness; preparation; alertness; — disposition (to); indication; threatening.
s.m. One who has a large income, &c. = āmad-wālā, q.v.s.v. āmad.
s.m. A mound or heap of earth on which a mark is fixed to shoot arrows at, target, butt ( = nishāna).
s.m. pl. (of makān), Dwellings, habitations, mansions, places.
adj. Without a necklace, not having a necklace on.
s.m. Hope, expectation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امل (amal), from Arabic أَمَل (ʔamal).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Clean, pure; bright, clear.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امل (amal), from Arabic أَمَل (ʔamal).

def: s.m. Register of one's doings or conduct; the register in which the deeds of men are supposed to be recorded.
s.m. Spirituous liquor, any intoxicating liquor or drug; intoxication
subs: s.m. Intoxicating liquor of any kind ( = amal).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian امل (amal), from Arabic أَمَل (ʔamal).

ety: inf. n. of عمل 'to work; to act,' &c.
def: s.m. Improper action; improper or bad use, misuse, abuse; misapplication, misemployment
subs: (-par), To pronounce a spell or incantation (over)
s.m. One who has command, or who exercises authority; an administrative officer; a (native) collector of revenue
s.f. Administration, government, rule, sway; lordship; limits of jurisdiction; the district governed, a collectorship
s.m. 'That which has come into operation'; a proceeding, process; — ʻamal-dar-āmad karnā, v.t. To put in practice, to act upon, to observe
v.n. To be put in practice, &c.
s.f. A warrant, a writ; a deed of conveyance; a certificate of title
(-par), To act (upon), proceed (upon); to operate, to take effect; to make use (of); — to affect the head (wine, &c.)
(-ko), To bring into operation or practice, put in execution, carry into effect; to make use of; to set to work
s.m. An order, a warrant, &c. ( = ʻamal-dastak); a code of instructions (cf. dastūruʼl-ʻamal).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عمل ('amal), from Arabic عَمَل (ʕamal).

def: (for A. عملة ʻamalat, pl. of ʻāmil, q.v.), s.m. pl. & sing. Workers, operators, executors; ministerial or public officers (attached to a court, or to an office), officials, functionaries; establishment (esp. the Persian or vernacular establishment of an office, as distinguished from the English department); — an official, a subordinate officer, a (native) clerk
subs: s.m. pl. Ministerial officers, officials; executive authority; clerks.
wety: From Arabic عِمْلَة (ʕimla).

def: s.m. Land near a mountain.
s.m. = āmlā, q.v. adj. Sour, acid; — s.m. Sourness, acidity.
adv. In act; in fact, indeed, truly.
adj. Practical (opp. to ʻilmī, 'theoretical'); to be done, fabrile, fictitious, artificial: — addicted to intoxicating drugs.
adj. Stainless, free from dirt, clean, pure.

def: s.f. Practice.

s.f. Disinterestedness, unselfishness; indifference, stoicism.
s.f. Security, safety; freedom from fear, ease of mind; protection, safeguard; promise or assurance of security or safety, indemnity, quarter ( = panāh), mercy, grace

(-ko), To give a promise of safety, to afford protection, &c.
To seek protection, to seek the assurance of safety; to ask for quarter
v.t. To keep in safety or under (one's) protection, to render (one) secure; to protect, defend, shelter.

def: s.f. A large oval mango (ām).

ety: S. आम्र+इन्, lit. 'of or relating to the mango'; but cf. āwan

ety: आ or अ+S. मानीयं, rt. मा
def: v.n. To go into, fit in, to be contained, be held.
adj. Not to be believed, not worthy of belief, incredible; not to be honoured, unworthy of respect.
adj. & adv. Aside, apart, untouched, intact, inviolable

s.m. Deed of trust or deposit; a document conveying anything.

def: adj. Deposited, given in trust or charge; — s.f. Any thing deposited, &c.

adj. & s.m. Incredulous; inattentive; — without pride, humble; — an incredulous person, &c.
